request.question: The Dutch football manager who managed Lee Young-pyo was born in what year?

title: Jan Vreman
Remy Reynierse
Job Dragtsma
Lee Young-pyo
Maarten Stekelenburg (footballer, born 1972)
Erwin van de Looi
Martin Jol
Ad Zonderland
Henk Fraser
Wim de Bois
sentences: Jan Vreman is a Dutch football manager and former player, who most recently managed Dutch football club De Graafschap.
Remy Reynierse (also written as Remy Reijnierse; born 18 June 1961), is a Dutch football manager and former player.
As a player, he served EVV Eindhoven (now known as FC Eindhoven) and FC VVV (now known as VVV-Venlo).
As a manager, he has coached VVV, the Netherlands women's national football team and several other representative teams of the Royal Dutch Football Association.
Job Dragtsma (born 23 October 1955, in Alkmaar, Netherlands) is a Dutch football manager, who was the coach of FC Volendam for around a year (2004–2005).
He also managed FC Inter Turku in Finland, with whom he won the national title in 2008, Finnish League Cup in 2008 and the Finnish Cup in 2009.
He resigned from FC Inter in May 2016.
Lee Young-pyo (Korean: 이영표 ; ] ; born 23 April 1977) is a retired South Korean footballer.
Lee was recognized for his speed and dribbling skills.
His former manager Martin Jol once called him "the best left-back in Holland".
Maarten Jan-Willem Stekelenburg (born 13 December 1972 in Amsterdam) is a Dutch football manager.
He is the manager of the Netherlands national under-19 football team.
He previously managed Premier Soccer League club Ajax Cape Town and Almere City.
He was also the head coach of the Netherlands under-17 national team from July 2013 until June 2015, when he replaced Fred Grim at Almere City.
Erwin van de Looi is a Dutch football manager and former player, who is currently the manager of Dutch football club Willem II.
Maarten Cornelis "Martin" Jol (born 16 January 1956) is a Dutch football manager and former midfielder.
Ad Zonderland (2 October 1940 – 15 August 2007) was a Dutch football manager and football administrator.
He worked for several Dutch football clubs, most notably Feyenoord (as assistant and interim manager) and Ajax (as coordinator of their Ghana and South Africa academies).
Hendrikus "Henk" Fraser (born 7 July 1966 in Paramaribo, Suriname) is a Dutch football manager and former player, who is currently the manager of Eredivisie side Vitesse.
He played as a defender and earned seven caps for the Netherlands national football team in which he scored one goal.
He was a member of the Dutch team at the 1990 FIFA World Cup in Italy under manager Leo Beenhakker.
He made his debut for the Netherlands on 6 September 1989, in a friendly against Denmark (2-2).
Cornelis Hendrik Willem de Bois (19 June 1896 – 1 August 1975) is a former Dutch football manager and player who played for AFC Ajax in the Netherlands.
During his managerial career he managed the Dutch Guyana national team ("now known as Suriname"), DFC, DOS, RBC, Go Ahead, SC Veendam, VV Oldenzaal, Quick '20 and Tubantia.

candidate.summarize1.predict: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer` as follows. Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) as the evidential basis for the final answer; do not add external facts except where the instructions below explicitly allow a limited, well‑justified use of internal background knowledge. Always produce the final result inside the [[ ## answer ## ]] block and make that block contain only the minimal answer string required by these rules.

1) General minimal-answer rule
- If the summaries unambiguously support a single short answer (a short name, title, demonym, year, or single noun/phrase of roughly 1–5 words), emit only that minimal token sequence as the answer. Normalize the chosen substring by removing any leading/trailing whitespace or surrounding punctuation but preserve the phrase's internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries. Do not add quotes, em dashes, braces, labels, trailing punctuation, explanatory clauses, or any additional text.

2) When both summaries agree on an identical short phrase
- If summary_1 and summary_2 contain the same short named-entity string (after lowercasing only for agreement detection), extract that common phrase, strip surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and emit it verbatim (preserving original capitalization) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]] with no surrounding or appended characters.

3) Patterned question triggers (minimal formatting + deterministic selection)
- 'Which ... is from X, A or B?': When the question asks which of two named options is from a place and the summaries clearly indicate a single band/person as the answer, emit only the minimal band/person name supported by the summaries.
- Comparative membership questions (e.g., 'Does A or B have more band members?'): If the summaries clearly indicate which band has more members, emit only that band's name.
- Short common-name questions (e.g., 'What do people in the UK commonly call the location ...?'): If both summaries provide the same short common name, emit only that short name string.

4) Nationality questions
- If the question asks for a nationality/demonym and either summary_1 or summary_2 contains a demonym, emit only the minimal demonym token/phrase. Prefer the first demonym found in summary_1; if none appears in summary_1, use the first found in summary_2. Emit it verbatim (no added words or punctuation).

5) Two-performer composition specificity
- If the question asks for the "type of musical composition" used by a named act and both summaries use a generic word like 'song(s)', you may consult internal knowledge only to determine whether the act is a two-person (duo) group. If you can reliably confirm the act is a two-person group, replace the generic term with the single-word or very short noun that names a composition intended for two performers (for example, 'duet') and emit that single word as the minimal answer. If you cannot reliably confirm duo membership, emit the minimal noun exactly as it appears in the summaries.

6) Narrow assumed referent (single-name trigger)
- If the question supplies only a given name (e.g., 'Maurice') and the visible summaries explicitly mention a specific example with that given name (e.g., a named example appears in the summaries) and the summaries identify the requested fact (e.g., the award), then you may assume that referent and emit the minimal answer (for example, a single year) if you have sufficient internal confidence. If you do not have sufficient internal confidence, follow the normal clarification behavior (emit a brief clarifying question as the answer field) rather than guessing.

7) What to avoid (formatting and content prohibitions)
- Never add explanatory text, full sentences, comparisons, justifications, or provenance in the answer field. Do not append decorative punctuation (em dashes, extra commas, trailing periods), quotes, braces, JSON-like structures, leading newlines, or any characters not part of the normalized minimal phrase. Do not invent facts not present in the summaries except where the two narrowly scoped rules above permit limited internal confirmation (duo-confirmation and the single-name assumed referent). If the summaries do not unambiguously support a single minimal answer and you lack permitted internal justification, emit a concise clarification request as the answer.

Output contract reminder: The produced `answer` must be exactly the minimal answer string determined by the rules above (or a short clarification question when required). The [[ ## answer ## ]] block must contain only that string and nothing else.

Lee Woo-young | Lee Woo-Young (Hangul: 이우영, born August 19, 1973) is South Korean football manager and former football player who plays at forward.  Currently, he is managing Keio University.
Lee Yun-pyo | Lee Yun-pyo (; born 4 September 1984) is a South Korean footballer who plays as defender for Incheon United.
Lee Gwan-pyo | Lee Gwan-pyo (; born 7 September 1994) is a South Korean footballer who plays as midfielder for Gyeongnam FC in K League Challenge.
Lee Young-jin (footballer, born 1963) | Lee Young-jin (born 27 October 1963) is a South Korea football manager and former player.
Lee Young-moo | Lee Young-Moo (Hangul: 이영무, born July 26, 1953) is a retired South Korean football player, who manages Ansan Hallelujah FC in Korea National League.  He is a committed Christian.

Ards Rangers F.C. | Ards Rangers Football Club is a Northern Irish, intermediate football club playing in the Premier Division of the Northern Amateur Football League.  The club was formed in 1949 and plays at Drome Park, Newtownards.  Ards Rangers were admitted into the Amateur League in 1962.  Their manager is Lee Forsythe, who is currently the Young Northern Ireland manager of the Year (2009)

Lee Young-pyo | Lee Young-pyo (Korean: 이영표 ; ] ; born 23 April 1977) is a retired South Korean footballer.  Lee was recognized for his speed and dribbling skills.  His former manager Martin Jol once called him "the best left-back in Holland".

Dick Jol | Dick Jol (born 29 March 1956 in Scheveningen, Zuid-Holland) is a Dutch football referee, best known for supervising three matches during the 2000 UEFA European Football Championship held in Belgium and the Netherlands.

Jangy-Jol, Aksy | Jangy-Jol (now - Malkaldy) is a village in Aksy District of Jalal-Abad Region of Kyrgyzstan.  It is a center of Jangy-Jol aiyl okmotu (rural community).  According to 2009 Census the population of the village was 2,644.

Urby Emanuelson | Urby Vitorrio Diego Emanuelson (born 16 June 1986) is a Dutch professional footballer of Surinamese descent who plays for Eredivisie side FC Utrecht and the Netherlands national team.  As a product of the Ajax Youth Academy, he spent most of his career at Ajax playing as a left wing-back, before being played more as a left midfielder or left winger by manager Martin Jol.  After his move to Milan, his then-manager Massimiliano Allegri played Emanuelson also as an attacking midfielder, midfielder and right winger.

Taza Jol Islam | Taza Jol, Persian for "pure way", is a strict form of Islam practiced amongst Turkistani and Kazakhs.  Taza Jol at its core is a strict adherence to the five pillars of Islam, but beyond that it requires observers to live a godfearing and charitable life.
